questions.question_text: How many clock hours of pre-license education must a salesperson candidate complete in Alabama?
questions.explanation: Alabama requires 60 clock hours of pre-license education from an AREC-approved school. The course must be completed within 1 year of the start date. This applies to both salesperson and broker candidates.

questions.question_text: A friend helps a homeowner find a buyer and receives a $500 gift card as thanks. Under Alabama law, this activity:
questions.explanation: Negotiating or facilitating the sale of real estate for another person for compensation requires a license in Alabama. The form of compensation (cash, gift card, etc.) does not matter. Even occasional acts for compensation trigger the licensing requirement.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state broker wants to co-broker a transaction in Alabama. Under the 2025 law, what is the maximum number of co-brokered transactions allowed per year?
questions.explanation: Under the 2025 co-brokerage amendments, out-of-state brokers are limited to a maximum of 3 transactions per year in Alabama, with a $50 million annual volume cap. An Alabama broker must supervise the out-of-state broker's actions.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate started their pre-license course on March 1. By when must they complete it?
questions.correct_answer: Within 1 year of the start date
questions.explanation: Alabama's 60-hour pre-license course must be completed within 1 year of the start date. If the candidate does not finish within that window, they must re-enroll and start over.

questions.question_text: Under Alabama's 2025 co-brokerage rules, which requirement applies to out-of-state co-brokerage agreements?
questions.correct_answer: Alabama broker must supervise the transaction
distractors: Only written notice to AREC is required
The out-of-state broker must be licensed in AL
questions.explanation: The 2025 co-brokerage amendments require that an Alabama broker supervise the actions of the out-of-state broker. Additionally, the maximum annual volume is capped at $50 million, and a portfolio of properties counts as one transaction.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a real estate license in Alabama?
questions.explanation: Alabama requires applicants to be at least 19 years old, which is Alabama's age of majority. Most states set 18 as the minimum, making Alabama's 19-year requirement somewhat unusual.

questions.question_text: A license applicant completed fingerprinting through Fieldprint Alabama 45 days ago. What is the status of those results?
distractors: The results are valid for 60 days
questions.explanation: Fingerprint results obtained through Fieldprint Alabama are valid for only 30 days. Since 45 days have passed, the applicant must be re-fingerprinted before the background check can be completed.

questions.question_text: An applicant who lives in Tennessee wants to get an Alabama real estate license. Is Alabama residency required?
questions.correct_answer: No, residency in any U.S. state qualifies
distractors: No, but residency in a bordering state is required
questions.explanation: Alabama does not require state residency. Applicants need only show proof of bona fide residency in any U.S. state. They must also be a U.S. citizen, permanent resident alien, or legally present in the U.S.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the Alabama real estate exam on June 1. By when must they apply for their license?
questions.explanation: After passing the exam, the candidate must apply for their license within 90 days. The exam itself must be passed within 6 months of completing the pre-license course. Missing the 90-day application window requires re-taking the exam.

questions.question_text: Which conviction could be grounds for the Alabama Real Estate Commission to reject a real estate license application?
questions.explanation: Under § 34-27-32(b), the Commission may reject an applicant convicted of a felony or a crime involving moral turpitude (dishonesty, fraud, deceit) — rejection is discretionary, not automatic. AREC decides eligibility case by case based on the applicant's record. Minor traffic violations and civil judgments for debt are not grounds for rejection.

questions.question_text: How many hours of post-license education must an Alabama salesperson complete?
questions.explanation: New Alabama salespersons must complete 30 hours of post-license education within the first 6 months of receiving their temporary license. Failure to complete on time results in inactive status.

questions.question_text: A new salesperson received their temporary license but has not completed post-license education after 8 months. What is the status of their license?
questions.explanation: If post-license education is not completed within 6 months, the license is placed on inactive status. If still not completed within 1 year, the temporary license expires and lapses entirely, requiring the person to start the licensing process over.

questions.question_text: A salesperson fails to complete post-license education within one year of receiving their temporary license. What happens?
questions.correct_answer: The temporary license expires and lapses
questions.explanation: At 6 months without post-license education, the license goes inactive. At 1 year, it expires and lapses entirely. The person must then restart the entire licensing process including pre-license education and the exam.

questions.question_text: Which type of Alabama licensee has NO post-license education requirement?
questions.explanation: In Alabama, the 30-hour post-license requirement falls on temporary salesperson licensees — new salespersons must complete it while holding a temporary license. Brokers have no post-license education requirement, though they must still meet continuing education requirements for renewal.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson wants to know when they must complete post-license education. The deadline is:
questions.explanation: Post-license education must be completed within 6 months of receiving the temporary license. Completing it on time converts the temporary license to an original license. The 1-year mark is when the license lapses entirely if not completed.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must an active Alabama licensee complete per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Alabama requires 15 clock hours of CE per biennial (2-year) renewal cycle. For salespersons, this includes 3 mandatory hours of Risk Management plus 12 hours of electives.

questions.question_text: An Alabama salesperson must complete which mandatory CE course each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Alabama salespersons must take a 3-hour Risk Management course as part of their 15-hour CE requirement. Brokers have an additional 3-hour mandatory Broker CE Course. The remaining hours are electives chosen by the licensee.

questions.question_text: A licensee misses the August 31 renewal deadline but renews on September 15. What additional cost do they face?
questions.explanation: Licensees who renew between September 1 and September 30 must pay a $150 late fee in addition to the standard renewal fee. On October 1, an unrenewed license automatically becomes inactive.

questions.question_text: Alabama's CE renewal cycle ends on September 30 of which type of year?
questions.explanation: Alabama's biennial CE cycle ends September 30 of every even-numbered year (e.g., 2024, 2026, 2028). The renewal deadline is August 31 without penalty, with a $150 late fee through September 30.

questions.question_text: A licensee on inactive status wants to renew. Are they required to complete CE before renewing?
questions.correct_answer: No, but they must pay the renewal fee
distractors: Yes, but only the Risk Management course
No, and no fee is required either
questions.explanation: Inactive licensees in Alabama do NOT need to complete CE to renew their license. However, they must still submit a renewal application and pay the applicable fees. They cannot practice while on inactive status.
